[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/Oshakati_street_market.JPG/250px-Oshakati_street_market.JPG)](/wiki/File:Oshakati_street_market.JPG)

The old open market of Oshakati at the [C46](/wiki/C46_road_(Namibia) "C46 road (Namibia)") (demolished in 2016)

**Oshakati** is a town in northern [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia"). It is the regional capital of the [Oshana Region](/wiki/Oshana_Region "Oshana Region") and one of Namibia's largest cities, both by population and as an economic center. It had a population of 58,656 people in 2023.

Oshakati was founded in July 1966 and proclaimed a town in 1992. The town was used as a base of operations by the [South African Defence Force](/wiki/South_African_Defence_Force "South African Defence Force") (SADF) during the [South African Border War](/wiki/South_African_Border_War "South African Border War").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

In [Oshiwambo](/wiki/Oshiwambo "Oshiwambo"), the language of the [Ovambo people](/wiki/Ovambo_people "Ovambo people"), the town's name means "that which is in between", although some believe that the name (Oshakati, also Otshakati) was used to refer to the broadcasting tower, which at 275 metres (902 ft) high was the tallest structure in the town centre and [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia").

On 19 February 1988, a [bomb blast](/wiki/1988_Oshakati_bomb_blast "1988 Oshakati bomb blast") at the First National Bank in Oshakati killed 27 people and badly injured nearly 30 others, most of them nurses and teachers. No one was ever convicted of the bombing, and the issue was dropped upon independence in 1990 in favour of national reconciliation.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Economy and infrastructure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy and infrastructure")]

Oshakati has experienced much development since [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") achieved independence in 1990. It was proclaimed a town in 1992.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Oshakati is located near the [B1](/wiki/B1_road_(Namibia) "B1 road (Namibia)"), Namibia's main highway, which stretches from [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa") through the capital [Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek") and on to the [Angolan](/wiki/Angola "Angola") border.

Since the turn of the century, there have now many shops like furniture stores, shoe stores, pharmacies, etc. The three main shopping centres are Game, Etango, and Yetu. The [Oshakati Independence Stadium](/wiki/Oshakati_Independence_Stadium "Oshakati Independence Stadium") is situated in town.

In April 2006, the Oshakati town council building was inaugurated by [Botswana](/wiki/Botswana "Botswana")'s president, [Festus Mogae](/wiki/Festus_Mogae "Festus Mogae"). Dr. [Frans Aupa Indongo](/wiki/Frans_Indongo "Frans Indongo") Open Market sells groceries, dried fish, and dried [Mopane worms](/wiki/Gonimbrasia_belina "Gonimbrasia belina").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

### Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Location")]

Oshakati is situated in the [Cuvelai-Etosha Basin](/wiki/Cuvelai-Etosha_Basin "Cuvelai-Etosha Basin") and is cut by the [Okatana River](/wiki/Okatana_River "Okatana River"). Both of these geographic features make the town prone to flooding; in 2008, it was last hit by heavy floods. The *Oshakati Master Plan Project* is underway to build a 23 kilometres (14 mi) dike around the town, to deepen and straighten the river, and to resettle people living near the riverbed and clogging the flow of water.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Demographics")]

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Climate")]

Oshakati has a [semi-arid climate](/wiki/Semi-arid_climate "Semi-arid climate") (*BSh*, according to the [Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification")), with hot summers and relatively mild winters (with warm days and cool nights). The average annual precipitation is 472 mm (19 in), with most rainfall occurring mainly during summer.

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Politics")]

Oshakati is governed by a town council that has seven seats.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The town area covers two electoral constituencies, [Oshakati East](/wiki/Oshakati_East "Oshakati East") and [Oshakati West](/wiki/Oshakati_West "Oshakati West").

Oshana Region, to which Oshakati belongs, is a stronghold of Namibia's ruling [SWAPO](/wiki/SWAPO "SWAPO") party. In the [2015 local authority election](/wiki/Namibian_local_and_regional_elections,_2015 "Namibian local and regional elections, 2015"), SWAPO won by a landslide (4,569 votes) and gained six council seats. The remaining seat went to the [Democratic Turnhalle Alliance](/wiki/Democratic_Turnhalle_Alliance "Democratic Turnhalle Alliance") (DTA), which gained 330 votes.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Major [Katrina Shimbulu](/w/index.php?title=Katrina_Shimbulu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Katrina Shimbulu (page does not exist)") from SWAPO has been serving in this position from 2007 to 2010, and again after the 2015 election.[[10]](#cite_note-back-10)

[SWAPO](/wiki/SWAPO "SWAPO") also won the [2020 local authority election](/wiki/2020_Namibian_local_and_regional_elections "2020 Namibian local and regional elections"). It obtained 3,028 votes and gained four seats. The [Independent Patriots for Change](/wiki/Independent_Patriots_for_Change "Independent Patriots for Change") (IPC), an opposition party formed in August 2020, obtained 1,887 votes and gained three seats.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Leonard Hango is[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit) the mayor of Oshakati.[[1]](#cite_note-reelected-1) Leonard Hango was reelected in 2024 as the mayor of the Oshakati Town Council.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Education")]

Many primary and secondary schools are to be found in Oshakati, e.g., Iipumbu, Oshakati, Ngolo, [Erundu Secondary School](/wiki/Erundu_Secondary_School "Erundu Secondary School"), Cabatana, and others, including Afoti Combined School in Uuvudhiya constituency in [Oshana Region](/wiki/Oshana_Region "Oshana Region") in the Omapopo cluster of Oshakati Circuit.

The University of Namibia is also found in Oshakati. The campus is located in the town center's Eliander Mwatale street, off the Main road C46, next to Oshakati Independence

The Oshana Regional Study and Resource Center was established on September 17, 2014, through the assistance of the [Millennium Challenge Account](/wiki/Millennium_Challenge_Account "Millennium Challenge Account") Namibia (MCA-N).[[13]](#cite_note-13) Situated between the GIPF house and the Social Security regional head office in Oshakati, the library can host up to 600 people, has 220 study spaces, a meeting hall that can accommodate 125 seated people, a video conferencing room, and shelving spaces for up to 35,000 books.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oshakati&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Culture")]

Oshakati town (popularly known as 'Otshakati tsha Nangombe' by the native Kwambi people) is within the Kwambi traditional authority.

Oshakati Town Council hosts the annual *Oshakati Totem Expo*. It combines the celebration of local traditions with a modern business exhibition.[[15]](#cite_note-15) The event was launched by Oshana governor [Clemens Kashuupulwa](/wiki/Clemens_Kashuupulwa "Clemens Kashuupulwa")on 9 June 2012. The four-day event now takes place annually in the months of June or July.

Oshakati has a football team, [Oshakati City FC](/wiki/Oshakati_City_FC "Oshakati City FC").
